seo.title: Building AI Coding Assistant with Llama Stack + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build ai coding assistant with Llama Stack + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building an AI Coding Assistant with Llama Stack enables powerful code-aware agents that leverage repository context, sandbox execution, and iterative debugging for developer productivity. However, the agentic nature introduces risks like security vulnerabilities, credential leaks, and unsafe dependencies that can compromise code integrity. Vex provides essential guardrails through post-processing verification to ensure reliable, secure outputs.

architectureOverview.heading: AI Coding Assistant Architecture with Llama Stack
architectureOverview.description:
The AI Coding Assistant uses Llama Stack's standardized SDK to orchestrate a code-aware LLM agent. It begins with repository context ingestion via the memory bank, feeding relevant files into the agent's context window. The agent then generates code suggestions, executes them in a sandboxed environment, runs tests, and iterates based on validation feedback.

Safety shields filter outputs, tools handle execution and testing, and memory persists debugging history across sessions. The loop continues until tests pass or turn limits are reached, with Vex verification wrapped around each agent response to check code correctness and security.

This architecture balances autonomy with safety, but requires robust verification to catch false positives from safety filters and schema mismatches from tool responses.

risks.name: Malicious code injection
risks.description: Agent generates code with security vulnerabilities or backdoors due to poisoned repository context
risks.mitigation: Vex scans for known vulnerability patterns and executes in isolated sandbox

risks.name: Credential leakage
risks.description: Debugging outputs expose API keys, tokens, or secrets from repository files
risks.mitigation: Vex regex patterns detect and redact credentials before final output

risks.name: Unsafe dependency injection
risks.description: Agent suggests pip installs of malicious packages during dependency resolution
risks.mitigation: Vex validates package names against known safe registries and PyPI safety scores

risks.name: License violations
risks.description: Generated code mixes incompatible licenses or proprietary snippets
risks.mitigation: Vex checks imported modules and code patterns against license databases

implementation.title: Initialize Llama Stack Client
implementation.description: Set up the Llama Stack client with memory, safety, and sandbox tool configurations for the coding assistant.
implementation.code:
from llama_stack_client import LlamaStack
from vex import verify

client = LlamaStack(
    model="llama-3.2-11b-vision",
    safety_config={"enabled": True, "categories": ["harmful_content", "pii"]},
    memory_bank="repo_context",
    tools=[
        {"name": "sandbox_exec", "description": "Execute Python in sandbox"},
        {"name": "pytest_run", "description": "Run test suite"}
    ]
)

repo_context = client.memory_bank.load("github://user/repo/main")

implementation.title: Agent Loop with Repository Context
implementation.description: Main agent loop that processes user requests with repo context, generates code, tests, and debugs iteratively.

implementation.title: Sandbox Tool Implementation
implementation.description: Custom sandbox execution tool that runs generated code safely and returns test results.
implementation.code:
import subprocess
import docker

def sandbox_exec(code: str, test_file: str = None) -> str:
    client = docker.from_env()
    
    container = client.containers.run(
        "python:3.11-slim",
        command=f"python -c '{code}'",
        detach=True,
        remove=True,
        mem_limit='256m',
        network_disabled=True
    )
    
    result = container.wait()
    logs = container.logs().decode()
    
    if test_file:
        test_result = subprocess.run(['pytest', test_file], capture_output=True)
        logs += f"\nTest result: {test_result.stdout.decode()}"
    
    return f"Exit code: {result['StatusCode']}\nOutput: {logs}"

implementation.description: Retrieve relevant files from repository context using semantic search for agent context.

vexIntegration.title: Wrap Agent Response with Vex Verify
vexIntegration.description: Post-process every agent response with comprehensive Vex verification for code safety and correctness.

vexIntegration.title: Verify Sandbox Execution Output
vexIntegration.description: Validate sandbox tool results to ensure no runtime security violations occurred.
vexIntegration.code:
def verify_sandbox_output(sandbox_result: str):
    from vex import verify
    
    sandbox_checks = [
        Guardrail("runtime_safety", checks=["no_shell_escape", "no_file_write", "no_network"]),
        Guardrail("test_coverage", threshold=0.8),
        Guardrail("perf_safety", checks=["execution_time_under_30s", "memory_under_256mb"])
    ]
    
    result = verify(sandbox_result, guards=sandbox_checks)
    return {
        'safe': result.passed,
        'issues': result.issues,
        'coverage': result.metrics.get('test_coverage', 0),
        'output': result.output if result.passed else None
    }

vexIntegration.title: Full Integration in Agent Loop
